name: Chocolate Hazelnut Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 1 cup Unbleached All-Purpose Flour, a pinch of salt, and 1/4 teaspoon baking soda.
2. Sift the dry ingredients together three times.
3. Rub in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Add the egg to form the dough.
5. Shape into a disk, wrap, and refrigerate.
Chocolate-Hazelnut Filling:
1. Toast whole hazelnuts at 350°F (175°C) for about 10 minutes until skins loosen.
2. Remove skins by rubbing in a towel or food processor.
3. Chop hazelnuts coarsely.
4. Melt chocolate with butter in a small bowl over hot water.
5. Combine corn syrup and sugar in a saucepan, bring to a full boil.
6. Remove from heat and stir in melted chocolate mixture.
7. Beat eggs and salt (and optional rum) until combined.
8. Stir in the chocolate mixture.
Assembling:
1. Lightly flour work surface.
2. Roll dough to 14-inch diameter disk, 1/8 inch thick.
3. Place dough in a 10-inch tart pan, trimming excess.
4. Mix chopped hazelnuts into filling and pour over crust.
5. Bake at 350°F (175°C) until set and crust is golden, about 40 minutes.
Holding:
Store the tart at room temperature for up to 2 days.
